api: List calendarView (expanded event instances over a time window)

required_query.startDateTime: ISO 8601; a value without an offset is interpreted as UTC — this exporter always sends explicit UTC values like 2026-06-01T00:00:00Z

rules: startDateTime and endDateTime are REQUIRED and are the only query parameters this exporter sends on the initial request; the fake rejects extras.
The range values are interpreted from the value's own offset and are NOT affected by the Prefer timezone header.

required_headers.Prefer: outlook.timezone="{Windows zone name}" — exact format including the quotes; controls the zone of start/end in the RESPONSE only; omitting it means UTC. Must be re-sent explicitly on every nextLink follow-up (custom headers do not propagate).

event_fields.subject: string
event_fields.seriesMasterId: string or null; occurrences point at their series master
event_fields.start_end: { dateTime: "2026-06-01T13:00:00.0000000" (7 fractional digits), timeZone: "UTC" | Windows zone name }

notes: calendarView EXPANDS recurrences: occurrences arrive as their own events with type=occurrence and a seriesMasterId; series masters themselves do not appear.
The same event can be replayed across page boundaries; consumers must deduplicate by id (first occurrence wins).

rules: Follow @odata.nextLink until absent. The $skiptoken inside it is opaque: reuse the link URL byte-for-byte — the fake 400s any rebuilt or re-encoded query.
Do not re-append startDateTime/endDateTime to a nextLink; they are already encoded in the token.
Wait exactly Retry-After seconds via the injected delay command, then repeat the identical URL.

At most MaxRetries retries per request. On exhaustion the exporter must not lose work: it writes a partial-progress file and reports status partial with pagesFetched/eventsExported/resumeLink/retryAfterSeconds diagnostics.

errors.distinctions: 401 -> GraphAuthError (no retry), 5xx -> GraphServerError (no retry), 429 -> retry per the throttling rules

rules: start/end are the wire dateTime strings with the fractional seconds stripped (2026-06-01T13:00:00).
timeZone echoes the zone Graph returned; organizer flattens to the address string.
Events are deduplicated by id and sorted by start, then id (ordinal).
range is {start, end} with the exact requested UTC strings.
Two exports of an unchanged calendar must be byte-identical regardless of the order the API returned events in.
